# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

## Vision

Our interdisciplinary program drives innovation at the crossroads of technology and
societal impact. Our mission is to cultivate a collaborative ecosystem where
knowledge, creativity, and ethical responsibility converge to empower the next
generation of innovators and leaders.

# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

- Anil Kumar Paidi
- Assistant Professor
- anil.paidi@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Anil Kumar Paidi

Currently serving as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Chemistry and the Interdisciplinary Program (IDP) at the **Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu**, **Dr. Anil Kumar Paidi** obtained his Ph.D. in Chemistry from **IIT Madras**, India, in July 2017. Following his doctoral studies, he pursued postdoctoral research in the Department of MEMS at **IIT Bombay**, focusing on the development of novel cathode materials. Subsequently, he worked as a Research Scientist at the **Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PAL)**, associated with **POSTECH**, South Korea, where he delivered outstanding research contributions in sustainable battery materials.

##### Research Interest

**Research Area:** Inorganic Solid State Chemistry and Structure Property Relationships, Development of Next-Generation Battery Materials.

**Research Interests:**

1.

##### Research Experience

**Assistant Professor**, Department of Chemistry and IDP, Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jammu, December 2024 – till date.

**Senior Research Associate**, Battery Materials Development Division, Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PLS-2), POSTECH, South Korea, June 2022 to Oct 2024.

PI: *Dr. Docheon Ahn* and *Dr. Sangsul Lee*.

Research topic: Design and development of sustainable battery materials for electrochemical energy conversion and storage.

Funding Agency: Al-based supercritical materials discovery funded by the Ministry of Trade, Industry & Energy, Korea.

**Research Scientist**, EUV Evaluation Technology Development Team, Pohang Accelerator Laboratory (PLS-2) POSTECH, South Korea, January 2021 to June 2022.

PI: *Dr. Docheon Ahn* and *Dr. Sangsul Lee*.

Research topic: Advancing battery material research with cutting-edge X-ray imaging technologies.

Funding Agency: Ministry of Science, South Korea.

**Postdoctoral Research Associate**, Advanced

# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

- Ankita Yadav
- Visiting Faculty
- ankita.yadav@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Ankita Yadav

Dr. Ankita Yadav is an Assistant Professor of Law at Dr. RMLNLU. She completed her LL.B.(Hons.) from Banaras Hindu University in 2013 and her LL.M. from Dr. RMLNLU in 2015, and she was awarded a silver medal in her LL.M. She cleared the NET(JRF) and All India Bar Council in 2015 and joined Dr. RMLNLU as an Assistant Professor in 2016. She also completed her Doctorate in 2021 and worked on Right to Privacy in India with a special focus on Digital Privacy. She also completed a short-term certificate course in 2019, organised by the International Union for Conservation of Nature. In April 2023, she was part of a round table meet on ChatGPT-Beginning of the End of Juris Copyright, organised by Hidayaullah National Law University. She worked as a trainee in the Corporate Law Department of Bharat Heavy Electricals Limited, Delhi and worked under the able gu

International, 2023.(Book)

Sons, 2025.(Book)

3. Ankita Yadav and Smita Patel, The Intersection of Forensic Science and Legal

Admissibility: Challenges and The Future of Evidence, Forensic Science and Forensic

#### Santhilal Subhash

#### Bioinformatics & Computational Biology (BCB) Laboratory focuses on improving female reproductive health by decoding regulatory elements in the genome using next-generation sequencing approaches.

**Key Focus Areas:**

- Reproductive Biology
- Genomics & Single Cell Biology
- Multi-omics
- Bioinformatics
- Computational Biology
- Bigdata Analysis
- Cancer Biology
- Developmental Biology
- Epigenetics
- RNA Biology
- AI/ML in Healthcare
- Biomarker Discovery

***Visit lab website:*** https://www.subhashlab.in

**Goal**

Three-year grant for post-doctoral studies in medical science. This grant aims to provide younger researchers with the opportunity to achieve qualifications leading to the establishment of independent research.

# Available Positions

We're inviting applications to join our Bioinformatics Team! We’re looking for passionate individuals who are eager to translate genomics into clinical applications. This position offers extensive exposure in analyzing advanced multiomic datasets from cutting-edge genomic technologies, as well as opportunities to engage in research and development and collaborate with scientists across India and internationally.

### Minimum Qualifications/Requirements:

Master’s (M.Sc./M.Tech. or equivalent) or Bachelor’s (B.Tech.) degree in Bioinformatics, Computational Sciences with an interest in Life Sciences research, Computational Biology, or Life Sciences. Candi

# Page Title: Interdisciplinary Program | IIT Jammu

- Sidharth Maheshwari
- Assistant Professor
- sidharth.maheshwari@iitjammu.ac.in

#### Sidharth Maheshwari

##### Research Interest

The information on internships and PhD vacancies can be found in the *Current Openings* section below.

General Research Interests: Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, Machine Learning (Tsetlin Machines)

Genome Analysis - We are embarking on an innovative venture to develop energy-efficient genome analysis solutions through hardware/software co-design, specifically targeting low-power and affordable platforms. The focus is on genome assembly and downstream analysis using data generated from 2nd and 3rd generation sequencing technology. In additions,  we intent to develop affordable hardware-based metagenomic and anti-microbial resistance monitoring pipelines

Battery Management System - Improving the battery management system (BMS) in electric vehicles (EVs) throug

##### Publications

1. T. Rahman, G. Mao, S. Maheshwari, R. Shafik and A. Yakovlev, 'MATADOR: Automated System-on-Chip Tsetlin Machine Design Generation for Edge Applications," *2024 Design, Automation & Test in Europe Conference & Exhibition (DATE),*Valencia, Spain. (Accepted)
2. S. Maheshwari, T. Rahman, A. Wheeldon, R. Shafik, A. Yakovlev, F. Xia, "Introducing TRIM Automata for Tsetlin Machines," 2023 International Symposium on the Tsetlin Machine (ISTM), Newcastle Upon Tyne, UK.
3. T. Rahman, G. Mao, S. Maheshwari, K. Krishnamurthy, R. Shafik, A. Yakovlev, "Parallel Symbiotic Random Number Generator for Training Tsetlin Machines on FPGA," 2023 International Symposium on the Tsetlin Machine (ISTM), Newcastle Upon Tyne, UK.
4. S.J. Ojukwu, S. Maheshwari, R. Shafik, A. Yakovlev, M. Mamlouk, "AI-Driven Battery State-of-Charge Estimation using Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy,"  2023 International Symposium on the Tsetlin Machine (ISTM), Newcastle Upon Tyne, UK.
5. S. Maheshwari e

- Parallel and distributed computing of computational genomic pipelines on emebedded cluster.
- RISC V based System-on-Chip Design for Computational Genomic pipelines.

I am actively looking for **PhD students** who would like to work in the following projects:

- Many-core RISC V based SoC for computational genomic applications. The candidate should be good in coding with fundamental understanding of computer architecture, and digital logic design.
- ML based Advanced Battery Management systems. The candidate should have Bachelors in Electronics and Electrical Engineering and should, also, have done courses on ML/AI.
- Molecular diagnostics for water quality monitoring and anti-microbial resistance surveillance. The candidate should have experience in performing wet-lab experiments required for generating genomic data using sequencing machines. This will be an interdisciplinary project between Department of Computer Science and Biosciences and Bioengineering at IIT Jammu.

## From The Desk of HoD

Welcome to the Department of Interdisciplinary Program at IIT Jammu.
Our interdisciplinary program drives innovation at the crossroads of
technology and societal impact. Our mission is to cultivate a
collaborative ecosystem where knowledge, creativity, and ethical
responsibility converge to empower the next generation of innovators and

Anil Kumar Paidi is a faculty member in the Interdisciplinary Programmes at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: anil.paidi@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: 1. **Ph. D.** Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Madras, Chennai, India. (2012–2017).
2. **M. Sc.** (Chemistry), College of Sciences, Sri Venkateswara University, Tirupati, India.. Research areas: 1. **Solid State Chemistry** - Investigation of Novel Oxides Synthesis, characterization, and development of structure-property relationships in new functional inorganic materials, 2. **Energy Storage Materials -** Synthesis, and Electrochemical study Alkali metal-ion battery systems, 3. **Investigating Electrode Materials in Real-time -** Employing state of art techniques to study electrode materials in real-time (in-situ).

Ankita Yadav is a faculty member in the Interdisciplinary Programmes at IIT Jammu. Designation: Faculty Member. Email: ankita.yadav@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: 1. PhD in Law, 2016-2021, Dr. RMLNLU
2. Master in Laws, 2014-2015, Dr. RMLNLU
3. Bachelor of Laws, 2010-2013, Banaras Hindu University
4. Graduation in Commerce, 2007-2010, University of Lucknow. Research areas: Digital Divide, AI and Data Protection, Judgement and Decision Making, Critical Thinking and Interpretation.

Avinash Raulo is a faculty member in the Interdisciplinary Programmes at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: avinash.raulo@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: 1. PhD, Indian Institute of Technology Delhi, 2016 - 2021,
2. M.Tech, Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur, 2012 - 2014
3. B.Tech, Central Institute of Plastics Engineering and Technology, Bhubane. Research areas: Energy Storage (Li-S, Li-ion, Li-metal and other rechargeable batteries, Supercapacitors), Material (Soft materials and Polymers, Inorganic and Carbon Materials, Conductive Composites, Biomass) for Energy Application.

Rahul R Salunkhe is a faculty member in the Interdisciplinary Programmes at IIT Jammu. Designation: Associate Professor. Email: rahul.salunkhe@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: **Ph. D.** in Physics (Thin film Physics) 2009.

**M. Sc.** in Physics (Solid state physics) 2005.. Research areas: Solid State Physics, Material Science, Nanotechnology, Ultracapacitor, Nanoporous Materials, Solid-state batteries.

Santhilal Subhash is a faculty member in the Interdisciplinary Programmes at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: santhilal.subhash@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - *2014 - 2020* - **Ph.D. in Medical Science (Bioinformatics & Computational Biology) | University of Gothenburg (Sweden).**
- *2004 - 2008* - **B.Tech. in Bioinformatics | SRM University (India).**. Research areas: Our lab at IIT Jammu focuses on understanding the complexities of female reproductive tissues, particularly in the context of gynecological diseases and cancers. This research emphasizes the significance of both coding and noncoding regulatory elements in the genome (REGULOME), which drive cellular heterogeneity and plasticity. By employing advanced Next-Generation Sequencing (NGS) techniques and computational models, we aim to map these regulatory elements to uncover mechanisms underlying disease progression and treatment resistance. The ultimate goal is to develop targeted therapies that address tissue heterogeneity and improve women's reproductive health, Key Focus Areas, Reproductive Biology, Genomics & Single Cell Biology, Multi-omics.

Sidharth Maheshwari is a faculty member in the Interdisciplinary Programmes at IIT Jammu. Designation: Assistant Professor. Email: sidharth.maheshwari@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: - PhD (2016-2021) Newcastle University UK
- B.Tech(2009-2013) Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati. Research areas: Genome Analysis, Hardware-Software Co-Design, Battery Management Systems, Machine Learning (Tsetlin Machines), Genome Analysis - We are embarking on an innovative venture to develop energy-efficient genome analysis solutions through hardware/software co-design, specifically targeting low-power and affordable platforms. The focus is on genome assembly and downstream analysis using data generated from 2nd and 3rd generation sequencing technology. In additions, we intent to develop affordable hardware-based metagenomic and anti-microbial resistance monitoring pipelines, Battery Management System - Improving the battery management system (BMS) in electric vehicles (EVs) through the integration of machine learning (ML) via hardware/software co-design at the edge holds great potential for enhancing the overall performance of EVs. By leveraging ML in the BMS.

Uma Satya Ranjan is the Head of Department (HoD) of the Interdisciplinary Programmes at IIT Jammu. As HoD, they lead the department's academic and research activities. Designation: Head of Department (HoD). Email: uma.ranjan@iitjammu.ac.in. Education: Ph.D (IISc), M.Sc. (Engg) - IISc, B.E. (TCE, Madurai). Research areas: Biomedical Data Analysis, Deep Learning, Reinforcement Learning.
